ontology.foodon_gap_note: No dedicated FoodOn class exists for croffles (croissant-waffle hybrid). Mapped to 'waffle food product' (FOODON_03315021) as the closest structural parent analogue; croissant dough lamination and toppings are not captured by this class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pastries and cakes) used as the closest defensible parent category for this croissant-waffle hybrid pastry topped with chocolate sauce and custard cream. No specific croffle subcategory exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for croffle or croissant-waffle hybrid food product.

preparation_method: Topped with chocolate sauce and custard cream
main_ingredients: Croissant dough (refined wheat flour, butter, yeast)

serving.description: 4 pieces of croffles with chocolate sauce and custard cream toppings
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 4 croffles (approximately 280 g total) topped with chocolate sauce and custard cream, as sold on GrabFood Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Croissant, butter (SR Legacy ID 18023) and Waffle, plain, prepared from recipe (SR Legacy ID 18361), combined with custard and chocolate sauce component estimates from USDA SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for croffles. Input per_100g values (energy 339 kcal, protein 6.4 g, fat 19.6 g, carbohydrate 33.9 g, sugar 16.1 g, sodium 196 mg, saturated fat 10.7 g, trans fat 0.54 g, dietary fibre 1.1 g, cholesterol 64 mg, calcium 43 mg, iron 0.89 mg) are internally consistent with a laminated pastry topped with chocolate sauce and custard cream. Values are cross-validated against USDA SR Legacy croissant (butter) and waffle entries and are retained as the best available estimate. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water are set to -1 as detailed micronutrient breakdown for this composite dish is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database for this specific preparation.

gi_source.title: Croissant and refined wheat pastry GI analogous estimate — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for croffles. GI of 72 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for croissant (GI ~67–75, mean ~72) and refined wheat waffle (GI ~76), weighted toward the croissant-dough base which constitutes the majority of the product. The addition of chocolate sauce and custard cream (high-sugar toppings) supports a high-GI classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (72 × 95.0 g carbohydrate in 280 g serving) / 100 = 68.4, rounded to 68.0. Available carbohydrate per serving = 33.93 g/100g × 2.8 = 95.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (16.1 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (1.1 g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium at 196 mg/100g is moderate but the sugar-fibre imbalance is the primary disqualifier
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid croffles due to the high sugar load (16.1 g/100g with only 1.1 g/100g fibre), which promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention and elevates blood pressure risk independent of the moderate sodium content; chocolate sauce and custard cream toppings should be omitted if any portion is consumed.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~72), high sugar (16.1 g/100g), high refined carbohydrate (33.9 g/100g), and very low dietary fibre (1.1 g/100g) produce rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Croffles are unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to their high GI (~72), high sugar content (16.1 g/100g), and low fibre (1.1 g/100g) from refined croissant dough, compounded by chocolate sauce and custard cream toppings that further spike postprandial blood glucose; this food should be avoided or consumed only in very small portions on rare occasions with close glycaemic monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 10.71 g/100g (exceeds 5 g/100g threshold) and trans fat at 0.54 g/100g from commercial laminated croissant dough; both are independent cardiovascular risk factors
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Croffles pose a high cardiovascular risk due to saturated fat at 10.7 g/100g and trans fat at 0.54 g/100g from commercial croissant dough, both of which raise LDL cholesterol and promote atherosclerosis; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid this food.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Trans fat (0.54 g/100g) and saturated fat (10.71 g/100g) from commercial croissant dough are strongly pro-inflammatory; refined sugar (16.1 g/100g) further promotes inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid croffles as the combination of trans fat (0.54 g/100g), high saturated fat (10.71 g/100g), and refined sugar (16.1 g/100g) creates a strongly pro-inflammatory nutritional profile with no offsetting anti-inflammatory factors.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Croffles are a contemporary fusion pastry popular in Singapore and across East and Southeast Asia, created by pressing laminated croissant dough into a waffle iron to produce a product that combines the flaky, buttery, layered interior of a croissant with the crispy, grid-patterned exterior of a waffle. This serving consists of four croffles (approximately 280 g total) topped with sweet chocolate sauce and creamy custard cream. The texture is characteristically crispy and caramelised on the outside from the waffle iron's direct heat, while the interior remains soft, airy, and richly buttery from the laminated dough. The flavour profile is sweet, buttery, and indulgent, with the chocolate sauce adding bitterness-sweetness contrast and the custard cream contributing a smooth, eggy richness. Nutritionally, croffles are energy-dense at approximately 339 kcal per 100 g, with high fat (19.6 g/100g, of which 10.7 g is saturated fat and 0.54 g is trans fat from commercial laminated dough), moderate protein (6.4 g/100g), and high refined carbohydrate (33.9 g/100g) with significant added sugar (16.1 g/100g) and low dietary fibre (1.1 g/100g). The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 72 (high), making this a high-glycemic-load food unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The high saturated and trans fat content also makes this food unsuitable for those with rheumatoid arthritis or cardiovascular risk. Croffles are classified as ultra-processed baked goods and are vegetarian but not vegan, halal status unconfirmed. They are commonly served as a snack or dessert in café settings across Singapore.
common_user_queries: What is a croffle made of?

confidence_reason: No exact approved-source entry exists for croffles as a composite dish. Per-100g nutrient values from the input (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are internally consistent with a laminated pastry topped with chocolate sauce and custard cream and have been cross-validated against USDA SR Legacy croissant (butter) and waffle entries. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for croissant and refined wheat waffle. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
